claim: One developer can effectively orchestrate and manage dozens of AI agents by focusing on high-level design and alignment rather than direct task execution
actionable: Design multi-agent systems where one human coordinates many agents; spend time on system architecture and monitoring rather than task implementation
source_title: Collaborative AI Engineering: One Dev, Two Dozen Agents, Zero Alignment — Maggie Appleton, GitHub

claim: Prompt engineering for multi-agent systems requires explicit role definitions, constraints, and communication protocols between agents
actionable: Define each agent's role, output constraints, and expected behavior patterns in prompts; use structured formats for inter-agent communication
source_title: Collaborative AI Engineering: One Dev, Two Dozen Agents, Zero Alignment — Maggie Appleton, GitHub

claim: In collaborative AI engineering, the human acts as an alignment keeper and orchestrator, ensuring agents stay on track with the overall goal
actionable: Set up monitoring and logging for multi-agent systems; create human checkpoints to verify alignment before agents execute critical decisions
source_title: Collaborative AI Engineering: One Dev, Two Dozen Agents, Zero Alignment — Maggie Appleton, GitHub
